summary: Pristine matrix recording of an epic 1983 show highlighted by a monster Sugaree and soaring Jerry solos.
review: Reviewers rave about the extraordinary performance – long, flowing jams like the nearly 20‑minute Sugaree, fiery Bird Song, and a stellar set of classics – all captured in crystal‑clear matrix sound. The treatment is praised as full‑bodied and pristine, making this release one of the top 80’s recordings despite one outlier complaint about a file fix.

ai_rating.rationale: All detailed reviews (5‑star or 4‑star) commend both the musical execution and the pristine matrix sound; only a single low‑rating comment concerns file fixing, not performance.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Full‑spectrum, crystal‑clear matrix blend; reviewers describe it as pristine, full and absolutely listenable with no noted distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert rides a wave of extended jams and tight transitions, delivering long versions that build energy from the opener through to the final Revolution, with the matrix capture preserving both stage mix and crowd ambience.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar work dominates – soaring, marathon solos on Sugaree, majestic runs on Bird Song and Fire‑on‑the‑Mountain (Friend of the Devil), and a glistening finish on Revolution.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir provides strong rhythm and vocal delivery, highlighted on the standout 'Good Lovin'' performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s organ/keys are praised throughout – ripping through Samson, Touch, and adding lush textures to many numbers.
